LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MIRA SINCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION AGAINST MIRA SINCO PENDING COMPLETION OF CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT'S DELAY IN SEEKING ARBITRATION IS UNREASONABLE. DEFENDANT HAS PARTICIPATED IN THE LITIGATION THAT INVOLVES OTHER PARTIES NOT BOUND BY THE AGREEMENT. ORDERING ARBITRATION AT THIS LATE DATE WOULD PREJUDICE THE OTHER PARTIES TO THIS LITIGATION, IN PARTICULAR PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MIRA SINCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION AGAINST MIRA SINCO PENDING COMPLETION OF CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION.
APPEARANCES: CAROLYN KENNEDY, ESQUIRE (OFFICES OF PAUL STEINER, 415,981.6100) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LEO M. LaROCCA, ESQUIRE (NIVEN & SMITH LAW OFFICES, 415.981.5451) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT'S DELAY IN SEEKING ARBITRATION IS UNREASONABLE. DEFENDANT HAS PARTICIPATED IN THE LITIGATION THAT INVOLVES OTHER PARTIES NOT BOUND BY THE AGREEMENT. ORDERING ARBITRATION AT THIS LATE DATE WOULD PREJUDICE THE OTHER PARTIES TO THIS LITIGATION, IN PARTICULAR PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J.
